description: The dataset '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/ExamAnxiety.csv' is already open in JASP. This dataset (Field, 2013) contains exam performance data with columns: 'Code', 'Revise', 'Exam', 'Anxiety', and 'Gender'. Perform an Analysis of Covariance (ANCOVA) to test the effect of 'Gender' on 'Exam' performance, controlling for 'Anxiety'. Your analysis should include descriptive statistics, estimates of effect size (η²), adjusted marginal means for Gender, and a test for the homogeneity of regression slopes. Save the JASP analysis file as '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/ExamAnxiety_ANCOVA.jasp'. Create a text report at '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/ancova_report.txt' containing the F-statistic and p-value for the Anxiety covariate, the F-statistic and p-value for the Gender main effect, the effect size (η²) for Gender, the adjusted marginal means for Male and Female, the p-value for the Homogeneity of Regression Slopes assumption check, and a one-sentence conclusion on whether Gender has a significant effect after controlling for Anxiety.
